description: Reference implementation: call public AIDP / OCI APIs from a custom code tool using an OCI API-key signer resolved at runtime from AIDP's Credential Store. No PEM files in the zip; no hardcoded keys in source.

tools.description: Demonstrates aidputils.secrets.get(...) + oci.signer.Signer(private_key_content=...). Validates the signer with op='whoami', then exercises the failing list_volumes endpoint with op='list_volumes'.

schema.name: op
schema.description: Which operation to run. 'whoami' calls the OCI Identity service to prove the signer works end-to-end. 'list_volumes' calls the public AIDP volumes endpoint (the one that returned 401 under resource principal).

schema.name: credential_name
schema.description: Display name of the SECRET_TOKEN credential in AIDP's Credential Store. Must have four secret keys: tenancy, user, fingerprint, private_key (PEM body, not a path). Leave blank to fall back to conf.credential_name.

schema.name: data_lake_ocid
schema.description: Data Lake OCID. Only used for op='list_volumes'. Falls back to conf.data_lake_ocid.

schema.name: catalog_key
schema.description: Catalog key (NOT display name). Only used for op='list_volumes'. Falls back to conf.catalog_key.

schema.name: schema_key
schema.description: Schema key (NOT display name). Only used for op='list_volumes'. Falls back to conf.schema_key.

auth.note: The agent runtime uses resource principal to call aidputils.secrets internally. The credential the tool then uses for downstream OCI calls is resolved per-invocation from the Credential Store — no permanent PEM in this zip.

credential_name.placeholder: Display name of the SECRET_TOKEN credential in AIDP
credential_name.help: Create the credential in AIDP → Settings → Credentials. Type: SECRET_TOKEN. Keys: tenancy / user / fingerprint / private_key.
